best_extraction.title: Comment on: Short pulse width widens the therapeutic window of subthalamic neurostimulation
best_extraction.journal_name: Annals of Clinical and Translational Neurology
best_extraction.study_question: Does charge-per-pulse reanalysis change therapeutic window interpretation in subthalamic neurostimulation?
best_extraction.population_assessed: Patients from Reich et al with subthalamic neurostimulation, reanalyzed from prior study data
best_extraction.outcome_measures: Charge per pulse, therapeutic window, rigidity control, muscle contraction threshold
best_extraction.inclusion_criteria: Reported Reich et al data across varied pulse widths
best_extraction.answer_to_study_question: Charge-based reanalysis indicates wider pulses yield a larger therapeutic window than narrower pulses.

reasoning: All models agreed on bibliographic fields except pages. Gemini's volume/pages matched journal-year plausibility best. Study fields describe a reanalysis/commentary, so laboratory_study fit better than case_study_uncontrolled despite low confidence. Outcome and conclusion were strongly aligned across models.

field_level_decisions.volume_issue_pages: Chose Gemini's 2:984-985 over Haiku's implausible 2015(1):984-985 and GPT's NAN.
field_level_decisions.study_design: Selected laboratory_study because article is a data reanalysis/comment, not a patient case report.
field_level_decisions.population_assessed: Merged consensus that prior Reich et al subthalamic stimulation patients were reanalyzed.
field_level_decisions.inclusion_criteria: Inferred minimal criterion from GPT extraction; others gave NAN.
field_level_decisions.number_of_participants_in_study: Set to 0 because this commentary reanalyzed prior data and no new cohort was enrolled.
